segments.text: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord seated on a throne, high and exalted; and the train of His robe filled the temple.
Above Him stood seraphim, each having six wings: With two wings they covered their faces, with two they covered their feet, and with two they were flying.
And they were calling out to one another:
Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of Hosts; all the earth is full of His glory.
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: At the sound of their voices the doorposts and thresholds shook, and the temple was filled with smoke.
Then I said:
Woe is me, for I am ruined, because I am a man of unclean lipsdwelling among a people of unclean lips; for my eyes have seen the King, the LORD of Hosts.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Then one of the seraphim flew to me, and in his hand was a glowing coal that he had taken with tongs from the altar.
And with it he touched my mouth and said:

Now that this has touched your lips, your iniquity is removed
and your sin is atoned for.
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying:
Whom shall I send? Who will go for Us?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And I said:
Here am I. Send me!
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: And He replied:

Go and tell this people,‘Be ever hearing, but never understanding; 
be ever seeing, but never perceiving.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Make the hearts of this people calloused; deafen their ears and close their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, 
understand with their hearts, and turn and be healed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then I asked:
How long, O Lord?
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: And He replied:

Until the cities lie ruined
and without inhabitant, 
until the houses are left unoccupied and the land is desolate and ravaged,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: until the LORD has driven men far away and the land is utterly forsaken.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And though a tenth remains in the land, it will be burned again. As the terebinth and oak leave stumps when felled, so the holy seed will be a stump in the land.
segments.speaker: God
